title: The method of fractionation of waste paper stock during defibering and separate fractions treatment
abstract: The subject of the invention is the method of fractionation of waste paper stock during defibering and separate treatment of fractions of high flexibility in selection of operating parameters which allows various types of raw materials of diverse defibering properties to be adjusted to, and which is at the same time energy-efficient, that is an important precondition in its usage in small paper mills for whom it is mainly designed. The process of waste paper defibering with its simultaneous sorting and cleaning is gradually performed during waste paper recycling. First, waste paper is disintegrated in the pulper into the form of suspension that obtains the ability to flow to other machines and devices. The machines and devices need to continue the defibering process - mainly of paper flakes and separation of heavy and light contaminants, for example foil.
claims: Claims Patent claims 1. The method of fractionation of waste paper stock during defibering and separate fractions treatment, in which released fibres and fine organic and mineral fraction, including microscopic particles of pigment-based paint as well as toners melted with the fibres structure can be separated and not subjected further to the activity of mechanical forces present in the pulper and the secondary pulper, characterised by that waste paper stock collected from the secondary pulper (2) and the pulper (1) is subjected during defibering first to fractionation in original pumping separators (4) with a conical screen, and then in pumping separators (12), whereby the short- fibre fraction discharged from the separator (12) to the vat (13) undergoes during the recycling process selective flotation and washing, whereas the long-fibre fraction after treatment in the screen (15) and the compactor (17) is introduced into the vat (19), and then through the pump (20) to the original multi-functional refiner (21), in which homogenous fibrillation refining and separation of toners from fibres and their shredding occurs. 2. Method according to Claim 1 , characterised by that the stock stopped by the conical screen of the pumping separator (4) is pumped back to the tub of the pulper (1). 3. Method according to Claim 1 , characterised by that in the scenario in which the pumping separator (4) collects the stock which has flown through the screen of the puiper (1), on the suction pipe (7) a closed heavy contaminants gravity separator is installed. 4. Method according to Claim 1 , characterised by that after the course of defibering in the puiper (1) is completed, the pump (10) feeds the stock to the centrifugal separator (11 ), preferably with a rotor with adjustable rotation speed adapted automatically to the stock consistency value, which is unusually located before the homogenisation and storage vat (5).
